---
title: "DRSLiterature Review2"
author: "Angela Rout"
date: "2025-10-11"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# R Script for K-Means Topic Clustering on Academic Abstracts (Plotly Interactive Visualization)
#install.packages("plotly")
# install.packages("htmlwidgets")

# 1. Setup and Package Installation
# Load necessary libraries
library(tm)        # Text Mining
library(tidyverse) # Data manipulation (e.g., tibble, piping)
library(cluster)   # Clustering algorithms
library(factoextra) # Visualization of clustering results (for Elbow method)
library(plotly)    # NEW: For interactive 3D visualization
library(htmlwidgets)
```

```{r}
# 2. Data Preparation (Simulation)
my_data <- read_csv("../data/raw/drs_merged.csv")
academic_abstracts<-my_data
```
```{r}
# 3. Text Preprocessing using the 'tm' package
corpus <- VCorpus(VectorSource(academic_abstracts$Abstract))

clean_corpus <- function(corpus) {
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, content_transformer(tolower))
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removePunctuation)
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removeNumbers)
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removeWords, stopwords("english"))
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, stripWhitespace)
  
  # Custom stop words
  custom_stopwords <- c("analysis", "study", "research", "using", "model", 
                        "review", "systems", "methods", "approach", "novel", "was", "will", "developed")
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removeWords, custom_stopwords)
  
  return(corpus)
}

cleaned_corpus <- clean_corpus(corpus)
```
```{r}
# 4. Create Document-Term Matrix (DTM) and Apply TF-IDF Weighting
dtm <- DocumentTermMatrix(cleaned_corpus)
dtm_reduced <- removeSparseTerms(dtm, sparse = 0.95)
dtm_tfidf <- weightTfIdf(dtm_reduced)
dtm_tfidf_matrix <- as.matrix(dtm_tfidf)

# Scale the data for K-means (Crucial step for proper distance calculation)
scaled_data <- scale(dtm_tfidf_matrix)

```
```{r}
# 5. Determine Optimal Number of Clusters (K)
print("--- Calculating Optimal K using Elbow Method ---")
elbow_plot <- fviz_nbclust(
    scaled_data, 
    kmeans, 
    method = "wss",
    k.max = 20
)

print(elbow_plot) # Uncomment to view plot
```

```{r}
K_optimal <-9
print(paste0("Assuming K_optimal = ", K_optimal, " (Please check the generated elbow_plot visually in your R environment)"))
```
```{r}
# 6. Run K-means Clustering
kmeans_result <- kmeans(scaled_data, centers = K_optimal, nstart = 50, iter.max = 100)

# Add cluster assignments back to the original dataframe
academic_abstracts$Cluster <- kmeans_result$cluster
academic_abstracts$ID<-1:nrow(academic_abstracts)

print("\n--- Final Abstracts with Cluster Assignments ---")
print(academic_abstracts %>% select(ID, Cluster, Abstract, Title))

```
```{r}
# 7. Analyze and Interpret Clusters

# B. Identify Key Terms for Each Cluster AND Generate Theme Labels
cluster_centers <- as.data.frame(kmeans_result$centers)
cluster_centers$Cluster <- 1:K_optimal
cluster_themes <- list() # List to store the generated theme names

```


```{r}
# Print the lookup table
#print(theme_lookup)

print(paste0("\n--- Top 5 Keywords per Cluster (K=", K_optimal, ") ---"))

for (k in 1:K_optimal) {
  # Select the center for the current cluster and convert the result to a numeric vector
  center_vector <- cluster_centers %>% 
    filter(Cluster == k) %>% 
    select(-Cluster) %>%
    unlist() 
  
  # Find the top 5 highest-weighted terms
  top_terms <- sort(center_vector, decreasing = TRUE)[1:5]
  
  # Generate a theme label from the top 2 keywords
  theme_name <- paste(names(top_terms)[1], names(top_terms)[2], sep = "/")
  cluster_themes[[k]] <- theme_name
  
  cat(paste("\nCluster", k, "Theme:", theme_name, "\n"))
  print(data.frame(Term = names(top_terms), TF_IDF_Score = as.numeric(top_terms)))
}
```

```{r}
# C. Look at the size of each cluster
print("\n--- Cluster Sizes ---")
print(table(academic_abstracts$Cluster))
```
```{r}
# A. Visualize Clusters (Interactive Plotly with Custom Labels)

print("\n--- Interactive Plotly Visualization ---")

# 1. Perform PCA (Dimensionality Reduction) on Scaled Data
pca_result <- prcomp(scaled_data, center = FALSE, scale. = FALSE)
# Get the theme labels for mapping (must be in order 1, 2, 3...)
theme_labels <- unlist(cluster_themes)
```
```{r}
# 2. Extract coordinates for the data points (Abstracts)
abstract_coords <- as.data.frame(pca_result$x[, 1:2]) %>%
  rename(PC1 = 1, PC2 = 2) %>%
  mutate(
    Cluster = factor(academic_abstracts$Cluster, 
                     levels = 1:K_optimal, 
                     labels = theme_labels),
    Abstract = academic_abstracts$Abstract,
    Title = academic_abstracts$Title
  )
```
```{r}
# 3. Project cluster centers onto the PCA space
# The centers are multiplied by the rotation matrix of the PCA result
center_coords <- as.data.frame(kmeans_result$centers %*% pca_result$rotation[, 1:2]) %>%
  rename(PC1 = 1, PC2 = 2) %>%
  mutate(
    Cluster = factor(1:K_optimal, 
                     levels = theme_labels, 
                     labels = theme_labels),
    Theme = unlist(cluster_themes) # Use the generated themes
  )
```
```{r}
# 4. Create the Interactive Plotly Plot
plotly_visualization <- plot_ly(
  data = abstract_coords, 
  x = ~PC1, 
  y = ~PC2, 
  color = ~Cluster, 
  type = 'scatter', 
  mode = 'markers',
  hoverinfo = 'text',
  text = ~paste(
    '<b>Theme:</b> ', Cluster, 
    '<br><b>Title:</b> ', Title
  ),
  marker = list(size = 10, opacity = 0.7)
) %>%
  # Add the Cluster Centers as text labels
  add_trace(
    data = center_coords,
    x = ~PC1, 
    y = ~PC2, 
    type = 'scatter', 
    mode = 'text+marker',
    text = ~Theme, # Display the generated theme name
    textfont = list(size = 14, color = 'black'),
    textposition = 'middle right',
    showlegend = FALSE, # Hide the centers from the legend
    marker = list(size = 15, symbol = 'x', color = 'black') # Optional: Add an 'X' marker for the center
  ) %>%
  layout(
    title = paste("K-Means Clustering: Abstracts Grouped by Theme (K=", K_optimal, ")"),
    xaxis = list(title = "Principal Component 1"),
    yaxis = list(title = "Principal Component 2")
  )
```
```{r}
# Print the interactive plot
print(plotly_visualization)

```

```{r}
# 8. Export Plotly Visualization to HTML
print("\n--- Exporting Plotly as HTML File ---")

# The saveWidget function from the htmlwidgets library saves the interactive plot
# as a self-contained HTML file. The plot can be viewed in any web browser.
html_output_path <- '../site/html/kmeans_topics.html'

saveWidget(plotly_visualization, file = html_output_path, selfcontained = TRUE)

print(paste0("Success! Plotly HTML file saved to: ", html_output_path))

```

```{r}
academic_abstracts_clustername<-academic_abstracts %>% select("ID", "Cluster", "Abstract", "Title", "Downloads", "Publication Year" )

cluster_themes1<-cluster_themes%>%as.data.frame()
cluster_themes1<-cluster_themes1%>%pivot_longer(cols=everything())
cluster_themes2<-cluster_themes1%>%select("value")%>%mutate(Cluster=as.factor(1:nrow(cluster_themes1)))
academic_abstracts_clustername$Cluster <-as.factor(academic_abstracts_clustername$Cluster) 
academic_abstracts_clustername<-academic_abstracts_clustername%>%left_join(cluster_themes2,academic_abstracts_clustername, by="Cluster")
colnames(academic_abstracts_clustername)[7]<-"ClusterTheme"
write.csv(academic_abstracts_clustername,"../data/processed/drs_merged_ClusterTheme.csv")
```

```{r}
table<-academic_abstracts_clustername%>%
  # 1. Group the data frame by the theme column
  group_by(ClusterTheme) %>%
  # 2. Summarise the resulting groups by counting the number of distinct titles
  summarise(
    Count_of_Unique_Titles = n_distinct(Title)
    )%>%
  # 3. Sort the resulting table in ascending order based on the count column
  arrange(Count_of_Unique_Titles) %>%
  # 3. Ungroup the data frame (good practice)
  ungroup()
```
